name: Italo-American
description_short: Member-owned federal credit union serving Italian-Americans since 1935, offering loans, mortgages, and share accounts with NCUA insurance up to $250,000.
description_long:
Italo-American Federal Credit Union was founded in 1935 by Italian-Americans to serve the Italian immigrant population of New York City at a time when traditional banks denied them financial services. The credit union has maintained its original mission for nearly 90 years, positioning itself as a community institution with deep historical roots in Italian-American heritage.

Italo-American FCU offers member loans at competitive rates, first and second mortgages, and share accounts/certificates typical of credit unions. As a federally chartered credit union, accounts are insured by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA) up to $250,000 per account. Operating hours are Monday–Friday 9:00 AM–2:30 PM and Saturday 9:00 AM–1:00 PM, with phone support at 718-366-6422.

What distinguishes this institution is its identity as a member-owned, not-for-profit cooperative with no outside stockholders. All earnings are returned to members as dividends after expenses, unlike for-profit banks. The credit union explicitly emphasizes its role as a preserver of Italian-American history and community identity, actively recruiting members from subsequent generations.

The main limitation is transparency: the website provides minimal detail about specific loan products, rates, terms, or membership eligibility criteria. The services page returns a 404 error, and there is no clear information about APRs, loan amounts, or application procedures. Potential members must call or visit in person to learn specifics, which may deter those preferring online self-service.
services: NCUA insurance coverage up to $250,000 per account
pros: NCUA insurance protection up to $250,000 per account—federal backing equivalent to FDIC for banks
Member-owned structure means all profits returned as dividends; no external stockholders extracting value
Nearly 90-year history serving Italian-American community, providing stability and community trust
Offers first and second mortgages and personal loans in addition to share accounts
Competitive interest rates explicitly stated as a priority versus traditional banks
Saturday hours (9 AM–1 PM) accommodate working members
Direct phone access (718-366-6422) and email support for member inquiries
cons: Website lacks specific rate information, loan terms, APRs, or loan amount limits—critical details missing
Services page (where rates/products would be) returns 404 error, indicating poor website maintenance
Membership eligibility criteria not stated; unclear if open to all or Italian-American/NYC-specific only
Limited hours (closes at 2:30 PM weekdays, 1:00 PM Saturday) may be inconvenient for full-time workers
No online account opening, loan applications, or digital banking mentioned; appears branch-dependent
best_for: Italian-American community members in New York City seeking locally rooted, member-owned financial services
Borrowers prioritizing relationship banking and personalized service over digital convenience
Individuals wanting mortgage or personal loans from a not-for-profit institution with 90-year local history
Members seeking alternative to for-profit banks with profits reinvested as member dividends
diagnosis: Italo-American FCU is ideal for Italian-American community members in the New York City area who value local history, member-owned governance, and personal service. The critical caveat is the absence of online rate information and detailed product specifications—prospective members must contact the credit union directly by phone or in-person visit to understand specific loan terms, APRs, and eligibility requirements.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Italo-American — Credit Union in Glendale, NY | CreditDoc
meta_description: Italo-American — NCUA-insured credit union in Glendale, NY. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
